title: keteleeria evelyniana subsp. xerophila
intro: Keteleeria evelyniana subsp. xerophila, a rare and endemic coniferous tree native to the mountains of Taiwan, is also known as the Taiwan fir. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Keteleeria evelyniana subsp. xerophila include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant's needles and cones are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to possess the same medicinal properties as the raw plant material.
